etymology.description: 度(度) (dù, "spend; measure") + 假(假) (jià, "vacation; leave") - to spend one's time on leave or vacation.
etymology.interpretation: 度(度) (dù, "spend; measure") refers to the allocation or passage of time. 假(假) (jià, "vacation; leave") is specifically the time taken off from regular duties. Together, 度假(度假) (dù jià) translates to spending one's time on vacation, emphasizing the idea of taking a break from routine activities.

examples.chinese: 我们打算去海边度假(我們打算去海邊度假)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen dǎsuàn qù hǎibiān dù jià.
examples.english: We plan to go to the seaside for vacation.

examples.chinese: 度假时(度假時)，我喜欢读书(我喜歡讀書)。
examples.pinyin: Dù jià shí, wǒ xǐhuān dúshū.
examples.english: When on vacation, I like to read books.

examples.chinese: 他们去了欧洲度假(他們去了歐洲度假)，游览了许多地方(遊覽了許多地方)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen qùle Ōuzhōu dù jià, yóulǎnle xǔduō dìfāng.
examples.english: They went on vacation to Europe and visited many places.
